class.name: ARSession
class.brief_description: Manages an AR session with world tracking, plane detection, and anchor management.
class.description: The main entry point for ARKit functionality. Supports world tracking, plane detection, scene reconstruction, hand tracking, image tracking, and more depending on platform. [b]Platform availability:[/b] - [b]iOS:[/b] Full support for all features including raycasting, world map persistence, collaboration, body/face/geo tracking, and camera image capture. - [b]visionOS:[/b] World tracking, plane detection, scene reconstruction, hand tracking, and image tracking via async providers. Raycasting, world maps, collaboration, body tracking, and camera images are not available (use RealityKit for spatial queries on visionOS). - [b]macOS 26.0+:[/b] Remote-session bridge only. Connects to a remote iOS/visionOS device and relays camera pose via DeviceAnchor. Anchor CRUD, raycasting, world maps, collaboration, and all other features are not available through the remote API.

method.name: add_anchor
method.description: Adds a custom anchor to the running session.

method.name: capture_high_resolution_frame
method.description: Captures a high-resolution camera frame asynchronously. [b]iOS 16+ only.[/b] Reports unavailability on visionOS/macOS.

method.name: get_current_world_map
method.description: Asynchronously captures the current world map for session persistence. The callback receives an [ARWorldMap] and an optional error string. [b]iOS only.[/b] Reports unavailability on visionOS/macOS (visionOS persists anchors automatically via WorldTrackingProvider).

method.name: is_supported
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] when ARKit session support is available on the current platform and device.

method.name: pause
method.description: Pauses the session and stops delivering new frame updates until it is run again.

method.name: raycast
method.description: Performs a raycast against detected surfaces. Returns an array of [ARRaycastResult]. [b]iOS only.[/b] Returns empty on visionOS/macOS. On visionOS, use RealityKit scene queries instead.

method.name: remove_all_anchors
method.description: Removes every current anchor from the session.

method.name: remove_anchor
method.description: Removes the given anchor from the session.

method.name: run
method.description: Starts or reconfigures the session using [param configuration]. [param options] is a bitmask of [enum RunOption] flags.

method.name: run_body_tracking
method.description: Starts the session with body tracking configuration. [b]iOS only.[/b] Reports unavailability on visionOS/macOS.

method.name: run_geo_tracking
method.description: Starts the session with geo tracking for GPS-anchored AR. [b]iOS 14+ only.[/b] Reports unavailability on visionOS/macOS.

method.name: run_with_world_map
method.description: Starts the session with a previously saved world map for relocalization. [b]iOS only.[/b] Reports unavailability on visionOS/macOS.

method.name: set_world_origin
method.description: Resets the session's world origin relative to [param relativeTransform]. Use this to realign virtual content without recreating anchors.

method.name: tracked_raycast
method.description: Creates a continuously-tracked raycast that invokes the callback as results update. [b]iOS only.[/b] Returns null on visionOS/macOS.

method.name: update_with_collaboration_data
method.description: Feeds collaboration data received from a peer into the session for multi-user AR. [b]iOS 13+ only.[/b] No-op on visionOS/macOS.

members.member: The anchors currently known to the session.
The last world-tracking configuration used with [method run] or [method run_with_world_map], if any.
The most recent frame produced by the session, if available.
A unique identifier for this session instance.

signal.name: anchors_added
signal.description: Emitted when new anchors are added to the session.

signal.name: anchors_removed
signal.description: Emitted when anchors are removed from the session.

signal.name: anchors_updated
signal.description: Emitted when existing anchors receive updated pose or geometry data.

signal.name: camera_tracking_changed
signal.description: Emitted when the camera tracking state changes.

signal.name: collaboration_data_received
signal.description: Emitted when collaboration data is produced for multi-peer AR. [b]iOS 13+ only.[/b] Requires [code]isCollaborationEnabled[/code] on the configuration. Never emitted on visionOS/macOS.

signal.name: frame_updated
signal.description: Emitted whenever the session produces a new frame.

signal.name: hand_anchor_updated
signal.description: Emitted when a hand anchor is added or updated. [b]visionOS only.[/b] Never emitted on iOS or macOS.

signal.name: interruption_ended
signal.description: Emitted after the session interruption has ended and tracking can resume.

signal.name: mesh_anchor_added
signal.description: Emitted when scene reconstruction produces a new mesh anchor.

signal.name: mesh_anchor_removed
signal.description: Emitted when a reconstructed mesh anchor is removed.

signal.name: mesh_anchor_updated
signal.description: Emitted when a reconstructed mesh anchor changes geometry or pose.

signal.name: session_failed
signal.description: Emitted when the session fails and provides a human-readable error message.

signal.name: session_interrupted
signal.description: Emitted when the session is interrupted by the system.

constants.constant: Reset session tracking when the configuration is run.
Remove existing anchors when the configuration is run.
Stop existing tracked raycasts when the configuration is run.
Reset accumulated scene-reconstruction data when the configuration is run.
